project.name: OpenKrakey
project.description: Minimalist microkernel autonomous-agent framework (TypeScript/Node). Per-Agent: clock + event-system + orchestrator(+context-buffer) + loader, wrapped by agent_instance. Global: boot + cli.

core.description: The whole framework — per-Agent runtime modules + their L1 contracts + shared modules + the two global modules (boot, cli) + the LLM communication gateway.

plugins.description: Phase-1 MVP plugins under public_plugin/ — the essential set that makes a bare agent converse, remember, and use tools, exercising every core function. Each implements the L1 plugin contract and talks ONLY via the per-Agent event-system.

clock.description: Per-Agent dumb timer: countdown + activate; rhythm controllable.

event-system.description: Per-Agent independent central bus: eventbus + actionbus; clock/loader/orchestrator/plugins all connect.

orchestrator.description: Per-Agent conductor; CONTAINS the context-buffer. Event-driven beat: gather (plugins refresh blocks) → compose → llm.request{context,messages}. compose splits blocks by target: system blocks → system prompt text (priority DESC, wrapped <label>), message blocks → messages array (groups concatenated by priority DESC, within-group order kept; the conversation is one such block). Dispatch tool calls from llm.return.

loader.description: Per-Agent plugin lifecycle: load private(override)+public (independents are no-copy: code from public_plugin/, dataDir redirected agent-private), build v1.1 PluginContext (leveled log + print + log.entry push), inject llm library, register (setup) into event-system, teardown.

agent_instance.description: Wraps ONE Agent: holds+wires clock+event-system+orchestrator+loader; receives the global llm library; start()->loader loads->orchestrator conducts; stop().

boot.description: Global startup ONLY: read each agent's config + config/llm.json, build the global llm library, build + launch each agent_instance. Composition root.

cli.description: Global standalone interactive (@inquirer/prompts) config tool: arrow-key pages to edit agent configs, the Default Setting, and the LLM communicator catalogue; Krakey logo; krakey/krakey agent/krakey default deep-links.

llm-gateway.description: Global LLM communication module: builds a key-less CommunicatorLibrary from config/llm.json (provider adapters: anthropic, openai-compatible; native fetch). Holds keys; does request+parse. Industry-standard infra, solidified into core.

llm-core.description: The LLM round-trip plugin: llm.request (carrying context + messages) -> communicator chat (key-less ctx.llm) -> llm.return (+ output.message for non-empty content — the monologue HOOK for observers, NOT a channel send). Reads the conversation from the event's messages (no conversation.get); emits llm.request.sent (the exact assembled request — system+messages+tools+params) for observers. Provides the llm.register_tool action other plugins call to declare ToolDefs.

persona.description: Stable identity context block (priority 10000+, stable-prefix convention) rendered from the agent's config.

system-prompt.description: Stable system-target context block (priority 9000, just below persona) introducing the LLM to the agent's OPERATING MODEL — the monologue rule + basic tool-use, CHANNEL-AGNOSTIC (never names a channel's send tool). Text/priority configurable. Identity lives in persona; this is operation.

web.description: Browser chat channel: a refcounted module-level node:http server (one per process); POST /api/agents/:id/message -> input.message + clock.fire_now; SSE /api/agents/:id/stream streams ONLY what the agent explicitly sends via its web.send_message tool (registered with llm-core; the orchestrator dispatches the call to web's action) — the LLM's output.message monologue is NOT auto-streamed. Contributes a `web.guidance` SYSTEM block (priority 8000, text/priority config-overridable) teaching THIS channel's own usage (Web Chat is a message channel; reach its user only by calling web.send_message) — the general monologue/operating-model rule stays in the separate `system-prompt` plugin. OWNS THE CONVERSATION via a `web.conversation` message-block (priority 5000) rendering its persisted transcript as clean turns (user input + agent sends; no monologue, no tool mechanics). The data-carrying plugin, so private-by-default (per-agent transcript). per-message sent/read status (read on llm.return). Serves a dependency-free static chat page.

inspector.description: Read-only browser debug/analysis dashboard: own refcounted node:http hub (loopback + token) + per-agent SSE; subscribes to ALL well-known bus events into a bounded in-memory record ring (live + scrollback); serves a dependency-free page showing prompts sent (the actual assembled llm.request.sent — system+messages+tools+params — with a readable/raw toggle) / received (llm.return), the event timeline, and detailed logs (incl. core:* lines), correlated per-beat by request id. Emits nothing — never perturbs the observed agent.

krakeycode.description: Coding-agent toolkit: gives a Krakey Agent real computer-use tools — krakeycode.read_file / write_file / edit_file (exact-substring replace) / bash (shell via cp.spawn; SIGKILL timeout; byte-capped output) / list_dir — declared as ToolDefs via llm.register_tool. Dual-mode security (local default | sandbox: root-confined + optional command allowlist; command timeout cap in both). Contributes a krakeycode.guidance SYSTEM block (priority 7000) describing the tools + current mode + pointing Krakey at the docs/PLUGIN_DEV.md authoring guide, and a krakeycode.results MESSAGES block (priority 4000) that folds tool.result outcomes back into the conversation on the next beat (plain user/name:krakeycode turns, not tool-role pairing) and nudges clock.fire_now.

searxng.description: Web-search tool: gives a Krakey Agent a `searxng.search` tool (registered via llm.register_tool) that queries a SearXNG instance's JSON API (GET /search?format=json) over native fetch with an AbortController timeout. Open-box endpoint resolution: an explicit config `instanceUrl` is authoritative (remote OR local); otherwise a local instance (http://localhost:8080) is tried FIRST, then a built-in pool of public JSON-capable SearXNG instances (overridable via publicInstances, gateable via usePublicFallback) — the first endpoint returning valid JSON with results wins, failures fall through, all-fail throws. NEVER spawns containers; no persistence. Contributes a `searxng.guidance` SYSTEM block (priority 6000) describing the tool + the next-beat delay + endpoint strategy, and a `searxng.results` MESSAGES block (priority 3500) folding recent search outcomes back into the conversation on the next beat (plain user/name:searxng turns, query in the header, newest-first total-char budget) and nudging clock.fire_now.

browser.description: Browser-control tool: gives a Krakey Agent READ+NAVIGATE control of a Krakey-MANAGED Chrome via raw Chrome DevTools Protocol over Node's global WebSocket (ZERO deps). Chrome is spawned via child_process with --remote-debugging-port + a dedicated --user-data-dir profile under dataDir, launched LAZILY on the first tool call, reused, relaunched on crash, killed on teardown. Tools (registered via llm.register_tool): browser.navigate, browser.read_page (text|html, char-capped), browser.list_tabs (no auto-launch), browser.activate_tab, browser.screenshot (PNG written to dataDir/screenshots, returns path — the LLM envelope is text-only). NO click/type/eval. One CDP WebSocket multiplexes commands by id with per-command timeouts + flat sessions. Contributes a `browser.guidance` SYSTEM block (priority 5500) and a `browser.results` MESSAGES block (priority 3000) folding tool.result outcomes back as plain user/name:browser turns on the next beat + nudging clock.fire_now. Mirrors the searxng/krakeycode tool-plugin shape.

config.description: OpenKrakeyConfig / DefaultAgentSetting / LLMConfig types + path constants.

theme.description: Krakey brand primitives (mint/dim/bold/red painters, ✦ glyph, ✔/✖ verdict lines) shared by every console surface (cli, boot startup report); degrades plain on non-TTY/NO_COLOR.

http-auth.description: Per-process session-token primitives shared by the loopback HTTP plugins (web, inspector): constant-time tokenOk (openWhenUnset knob) + Bearer/query/cookie extractors (cookie decode knob, fail-closed). Plugins keep their own source precedence + token-from-config policy.
